title: Resting Place
lines: Thinking for a moment that there might be more supplies here, you pry open a heavy, iron-strapped trunk in the corner of the attic.
The smell of old ammonia and formaldehyde hits you instantly. Inside is not food or tools, but a pale, twitching thing with too many limbs.
It lunges out, wrapping its cold, grey appendages around your head before you can slam the lid shut.

options.desc: Wake up back at the tent
